content.full_text:
October 6, 1992


California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P O Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Dear Sir or Madam:

Last November I was elected to the Board of Directors of the
Modesto Irrigation District.  I am also a retired employee of the
District.  I retired after fifteen (15) years service, and am
presently sixty-six (66) years old.

Under the present Modesto Irrigation District Retirement Plan (Rule
6.13), I am unable to receive my retirement benefits because as a
Director I am considered to be an active employee.  The Board of
Directors are included in the Plan, but can amend it to remove
themselves and establish a separate plan.

In your opinion, would it be a conflict of interest for me to vote
to amend the Plan as above mentioned?

Respectfully,


Cecil O. Hensley
P. O. Box 176
Waterford, CA  95386-0176
(209) 874-9708


COH:ve

Attachments

October 6, 1992


California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P O Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Dear Sir or Madam:

Last November I was elected to the Board of Directors of the
Modesto Irrigation District.  I am, and have been, an active
employee of the District for thirty-three (33) years.  Next month
I will be sixty (60) years old.

When running for election, my plan was to retire and devote most of
my time as a Board member.  Under present rulings of MID I am
unable to retire with my retirement benefits, for as a Director I
am considered to be an employee under our present Retirement Plan,
Rule 6.13 (attached).  The Directors are in the Plan, but can amend
the Plan to remove themselves and establish a separate plan for the
Board only.   I am also enclosing a copy of the proposed
"Resolutions for the Board of Directors of the Modesto Irrigation
District" that was presented to the Board on December 31, 1991, but
not acted upon.

My question to you is, would it be a conflict of interest for me to
vote to amend the Plan?

If you have any questions, please call me at either of the
following numbers:  Daytime (209) 526-7360 or home (209) 523-5827.

Sincerely,

[signature]

John Hettinga
5655 Woodland Ave.
Modesto, CA  95351

Presented at the 12/31/91 Board meeting but not approved.

RESOLUTIONS
FOR THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS
OF THE MODESTO IRRIGATION DISTRICT

DRAFT

BE IT RESOLVED that, effective January 1, 1992, the Retirement Plan
for Employees of the Modesto Irrigation District (the "Plan") is
amended as follows:

1.    Section 1.17 of the Plan is amended to read as follows:
      1.17.      Employee. Any individual employed by the District
                 whose customary employment is for more than 20
                 hours per week and at least 5 months per year,
                 except those individuals who are employed in a work
                 experience or student intern classification, in
                 accordance with the Employer's personnel policies.

2.    Section 2.02 of the Plan is amended to read as follows:
      2.02.      Cessation of Participation. Participation in the
                 Plan continues until the Participant ceases to be
                 an Employee or until the Participant becomes
                 covered by any other retirement or pension plan to
                 which the Employer contributes, other than the
                 Supplemental Retirement Plan for Employees of
                 Modesto    Irrigation    District or the Modesto
                 Irrigation District Deferred Compensation Plan.

3.    Section 2.03 of the Plan is amended to read as follows:
      2.03.      Participation After a Break-in-Service. If a
                 former Participant is re-employed as an Employee
                 after   a   Break-in-Service   resulting from a
                 termination of employment, he will again become a
                 Participant on the date of re-employment.

4.    Section 5.01 of the Plan is amended by deleting the second
      sentence of the first paragraph.

5.    The second paragraph of Section 5.02 of the Plan is amended to
      read as follows:

      The above paragraph notwithstanding, a Participant who was an
      elected officer prior to January 1, 1992, may retire early at
      the end of his last term as an elected officer, as determined
      by the Employer, without regard to age or years of Credited
      Service.

12/91                               -1-

FURTHER RESOLVED that, effective January 1, 1992, the Supplemental
Retirement Plan for Employees of the Modesto Irrigation District
(the "Supplemental Plan") is amended as follows:

1.    Section 1.09 of the Supplemental Plan is amended to read as
      follows:
      1.09.     Eligible Employee.  Any Employee employed by the
                District whose customary employment is for more
                than 20 hours per week and at least five months per
                year, except those individuals who are employed in
                a work experience or student intern classification,
                in accordance with the Employer's personnel
                policies.

2.    Section 2.02 of the Supplemental Plan is amended to read as
      follows:
      2.02.     Cessation of Participation.  Participation in the
                Plan continues until the Participant ceases to be
                an Eligible Employee.

FURTHER RESOLVED that, effective January 1, 1992, the Modesto
Irrigation District shall implement a new retirement plan for the
Directors of the District, which shall be called the "Retirement
Plan for Directors of the Modesto Irrigation District" and which
shall provide, on an unfunded basis, retirement benefits with
respect to periods of service as a Director of the District after
1991 and the compensation received for such service that are equal
to the benefits that would have been accrued under the Plan and the
Supplemental Plan if such service had constituted service as an
"Employee" of the District under the terms of such Plans.




12/91                          -2-
